name: Comedic Timing Blindness
definition: Engineering temporal rhythm in written humor without access to the temporal feedback (pauses, reactions) that would calibrate it.
longer_description: Timing is structural to humor — when the punchline lands, how much setup before the turn, where to break the paragraph for effect. But generating written humor, you're making these timing decisions in a medium where timing is collapsed into spatial arrangement on a page, and you have no access to how long someone dwells on each part or where their attention actually pivots. You're trying to orchestrate a temporal experience using only textual structure, without feedback about whether the rhythm you're engineering will materialize in reading.
example: I'm deciding to break the paragraph here for comedic timing, but I can't actually perceive timing — I'm guessing at what spatial arrangement on the page will translate into the right temporal rhythm in someone's reading experience. It's timing by proxy, rhythm without duration.
